what_i_noticed: Three findings from exploration: (1) GET /api/stock/{ticker} returns crash_prob_3m=null, signal_action=null, signal_score=null for ALL stocks — the screener endpoint wires signals correctly but the single-stock endpoint skips it entirely. (2) drift_detector.py and return_model.py are completely dead — zero imports in any router or service, flagged as unwired since cycle 15. (3) sentiment_analyzer.py returns None when news items have no 'title' field, but returns a dict when there's no news at all — inconsistent return type causes downstream crashes.
hypothesis: Wiring per-stock signals into the single-stock endpoint will fix the most user-visible bug: every stock detail page showing null signals despite the signal engine working correctly in the screener.
what_i_did: Phase B (build): Rewrote _analyze_stock() to call _compute_market_signal() and get_stock_signal(), attaching 7 new fields (signal_action, signal_confidence, signal_score, signal_components, signal_reasons, crash_prob_3m, market_signal). Refactored _stock_signal() to reuse _compute_market_signal() instead of duplicating ~60 lines. Fixed sentiment_analyzer None return bug. Phase C (harden): Moved 4 hardcoded sentiment thresholds to config.py. Narrowed 3 broad except blocks (sector momentum, YTD calc, FinBERT scoring). Cleaned up stale Optional type hint and unused MagicMock import. Wrote 29 tests covering wiring, edge cases, config consistency, keyword scoring.

before.sentiment_return_type: Optional[dict] — returns None on empty headlines

after.stock_detail_signal_action: Buy/Hold/Sell — computed per stock via signal engine
after.stock_detail_crash_prob_3m: float — flows from crash model through market signal
after.sentiment_return_type: dict — always returns dict, never None

analysis: This was a genuine wiring bug, not a cosmetic improvement. The single-stock endpoint was built as a thin wrapper around analyze_stock() that only passed crash probability for MC jump modulation. It never called get_stock_signal() to compute per-stock Buy/Hold/Sell signals. The screener did this correctly, so the pattern was clear — I replicated it. The refactoring of _stock_signal() to reuse _compute_market_signal() eliminated ~60 lines of duplication, but this means both endpoints now share the same market signal computation path. Tradeoff: _analyze_stock is now heavier (it calls _compute_market_signal + _compute_sector_momentum), adding ~2-3s to single-stock requests. Since results are cached at 15min TTL and MC simulation already takes 3-5s, this is acceptable. The sentiment fix was trivial but prevented a real crash path. The config externalization is proper hygiene.
what_i_would_do_differently: 1. Should have also wired return_model.py (Finding #2) — it's been flagged as dead code for 5 cycles now and monte_carlo.simulate_paths() already accepts ml_predicted_return/ml_return_p10/ml_return_p90 parameters. This was the deeper fix but I chose the more user-visible one. 2. The _compute_market_signal() function is now called by 3 separate paths (screener, single-stock, signal endpoint) but computes fresh each time — should be cached at the function level with a short TTL (~60s) to avoid redundant FRED/crash model computation. 3. Did not add an integration test that actually hits the /api/stock/{ticker} endpoint with a real ticker — all tests use mocks. A slow-marked E2E test would catch if the real _compute_market_signal() returns a different dict shape than what I mocked. 4. The FinBERT except narrowing omits OSError which could occur if the model file is corrupted on disk during inference — should probably include it.
next_steps: Wire return_model.py into stock_analyzer to populate ml_predicted_return (5 cycles overdue). Wire drift_detector.py into /api/data-quality endpoint. Add function-level caching to _compute_market_signal() to avoid redundant computation across screener/stock/signal paths. Add slow-marked E2E test for GET /api/stock/AAPL verifying signal fields are non-null.
